title: UC-010 three image styles (photoreal / illustration / cartoon) — cold end-to-end 1024px generation on Arc 140V, production path (#703)
question: Through the real BlarAI image_gen path with PRODUCTION safety settings (signed nested manifest verified + cartoon LoRA SHA-256 pin), what is the cold end-to-end (lazy pipeline load + INT8 GPU compile + 30-step generate) latency for each of the three shipped image styles on the Arc 140V, and do the three styles produce visibly distinct output? Captured for the #703 illustrate/cartoon feature go-live.

software.runtime: shared.inference.image_gen (BlarAI production engine) via ov_genai.Text2ImagePipeline, BlarAI runtime .venv (Python 3.11)
software.production_settings: require_signed_manifest=true (detached manifest.json.sig verified at load) + cartoon LoRA SHA-256 integrity pin b4c8132f...fe89 verified before runtime apply

illustration.name: Stable Diffusion XL 1.0 base (NO finetune, NO LoRA)
illustration.adapter: none - flat-vector look comes from the prompt template alone

cartoon.name: Stable Diffusion XL 1.0 base + DoctorDiffusion vector LoRA
cartoon.adapter: DD-vector-v2.safetensors (228,456,908 bytes / ~218 MB), applied at RUNTIME via ov_genai.AdapterConfig (alpha 0.8), NEVER fused into the base weights

method: device=GPU (Arc 140V), 1024x1024, 30 steps, EULER_ANCESTRAL_DISCRETE scheduler, guidance_scale 7.0, hires-fix disabled. Each style configured fresh, generated ONE image, then image_gen.unload() before the next style (the per-generate eviction path is exercised). So each reported latency is COLD end-to-end = lazy pipeline load + INT8 GPU compile + 30-step generate, single run (NOT averaged). Prompts: photoreal='a golden retriever puppy sitting in a sunny meadow'; illustration/cartoon='vector illustration of a coffee cup with steam, flat design, bold outlines, solid color background'.
findings: All three styles generated successfully through the production path; the signed nested manifest verified for every model and the cartoon LoRA SHA-256 pin verified before the runtime adapter was applied (is_available=true for all three).

Cartoon is the slowest (60.6 s) because the runtime LoRA application adds a compile/blend cost on top of the base SDXL load - this is the price of NOT fusing (fusing the LoRA into the base weights collapses prompt conditioning on this INT8 pipeline, the #703 root-cause finding, so the runtime-adapter path is deliberate).

Output PNG size tracks visual style: cartoon 237 KB (flat minimalist, high compression) < illustration 680 KB (flat-vector, bold fills) < photoreal 1.46 MB (high-entropy photographic detail). The byte sizes corroborate three genuinely distinct styles, not three renders of one model.
Illustration (base SDXL + flat prompt, no adapter) is the FASTEST at 39.8 s - no finetune-load nor LoRA-apply overhead beyond the base model.
not_measured: Steady-state generate-ONLY latency: these are COLD numbers (load + INT8 compile + generate per style, because each style is evicted between runs). Prior UC-010 entries measured generate-only ~10.7 s at 1024px; the steady-state per-style generate here would be similar, but was not isolated this run.
Resident RAM footprint this run (not sampled; the #666 Phase-0 spike and the 2026-06-24 Playground entry carry the memory math).
INT8 vs FP16 quality/speed not compared.
Image quality not benchmarked numerically; subjective verdict (operator + author): all three on-target and distinct.
